titleStmt.title: SII 11.58: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 58. (B.K. No. 44 of 1927-28). NĀGARAHAḶḶI, MUNDARGI PETHA, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ROUND THE PEDESTAL OF THE LIṄGA SURROUNDED BY FIVE IMAGES OF BULLS IN THE TEMPLE OF PAÑCHALIṄGA.

summary.p: The inscription which belongs to the reign of Jagadēkamalladēva (I) mentions the Mahāsāmanta Dasarasa entitled ‘Siṅgana Garuḍa’ i.e., the eagle of Siṅga (i.e., Jayasiṁha II,) as governing Māseyavāḍi One Hundred and Forty. It registers a gift of land made by the king and Nāgama-Gāvuṇḍa of Aragilvāḷa situated on the bank of the Tuṅgabhadrā river, to the temple of Dharmēśvara constructed by Tribhuvana-Nārāyaṇa Dharma-Seṭṭi. Since Dasarasa is called a son (magam) of Jagadēkamalladēva he may be taken to be a favourite servant of the king, not hitherto known from inscriptions.
The record is dated Śaka 944, Dundubhi, Chaitra, śu. 5, Sunday which is equivalent to A.D. 1022, March 10, Saturday. Sunday is probably a mistake for Saturday.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti . samastabhuvanāśraya śrīprithvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēśvara paramabhaṭṭāraka satyāśrayakuḷatiḷaka cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrījagadēkamalladēvara vijayarājya muttarōttarābhivṛddhipravarddhamāna mācaṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ saluttamire tatpādapadmōpajīvi samadhigatapañca
mahāśabda mahāsāmantaṁ siṅgana garuḍaṁ śrījagadēkamalladēvara magaṁ śrīmaddasarasaru māseyavāḍi nūṟanālvattama nāḷuttumire . sakavarṣa 944 neya duṁdubhi saṁvatsarada caitrasudda paṁcami Ādityavāradandu tuṅgabhadreya taṭi yaragilvāḷa nāgamagā vuṇḍanu marasumiḻdu tribhuvananārāya
ṇadharmma seṭṭiya māḍisida dharmmēsvarakke biṭṭa keyyi matta 6 . baredaṁ Īsarayya . After a little blank space below this is engraved © svasti śrī keḷallenadaṁ madura seṭṭi sagarayyana sa[yu]jjayya maga tribhuvana narayyaṇa dharma seṭṭi
Digital edition of SII 11.58 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
